name: Nsc (Naca Counseling Subsidiary) - Upland, Ca
description_short: NACA (Neighborhood Assistance Corporation of America) offers character-based mortgages with no down payment, no closing costs, and below-market rates, prioritizing homeownership access for underserved communities.
description_long:
NACA was founded in 1988 by Boston Hotel Workers Union members negotiating the first-ever housing trust fund. It has evolved into a national non-profit organization dedicated to closing the racial wealth disparity gap through affordable homeownership programs. Over 36 years, NACA has served 3 million people and helped over 500,000 homeowners achieve homeownership through character-based lending practices that prioritize borrower circumstances over credit scores.

NACA offers a flagship mortgage product called the Best in America Mortgage, featuring no down payment required, no closing costs or fees, no mortgage insurance, no consideration of credit score in underwriting, and below-market fixed rates. As of April 2026, their rates are 5.625% for 30-year mortgages, 5.125% for 20-year, and 5% for 15-year terms. The organization has issued 75,000 NACA mortgages and maintains $20 billion in mortgage commitments with partner lenders. Members access services through Achieve the Dream Events held across multiple states, where applicants can meet directly with NACA staff to become NACA Qualified.

What distinguishes NACA is their mission-driven approach to lending equity. Rather than traditional credit scoring, they evaluate applicants based on character and financial capacity, making homeownership accessible to low-to-moderate income individuals and communities of color historically excluded from conventional lending. NACA also provides HUD housing counseling (30% of all HUD housing counseling nationwide) and actively advocates against predatory lending practices and corporate real estate investors.

The primary caveat is that NACA membership requires attending in-person Achieve the Dream Events (2-3 day regional conferences) and completing their qualification process before mortgage approval. The organization operates as both a lender facilitator and advocacy group, meaning individual loan terms depend on partner lender relationships. Their focus on underserved communities, while admirable, means this is not a mainstream lender and requires active member participation in their mission.
pros: No down payment required on mortgages
No closing costs or fees charged
Credit score not considered in underwriting decisions
Below-market fixed interest rates (5.625% for 30-year as of April 2026)
Character-based lending approach accommodates non-traditional borrowers
Provides HUD-approved housing counseling (30% of national HUD counseling)
Established 36-year track record with 500,000+ homeowners helped
cons: Membership requires attending in-person Achieve the Dream Events (2-3 day commitment)
Limited geographic availability tied to scheduled event locations
Qualification process appears lengthy and requires active member participation
Must become NACA Qualified before mortgage approval eligibility
Not a direct lender; relies on partner lender network, which may affect terms and availability
best_for: Low-to-moderate income borrowers unable to save for down payments
First-time homebuyers with limited credit history or damaged credit scores
Borrowers in historically underserved communities and communities of color
People seeking below-market mortgage rates without traditional lending requirements
diagnosis: NACA is best for low-to-moderate income borrowers and first-time homebuyers seeking affordable mortgages without traditional credit requirements, but success requires attending mandatory in-person Achieve the Dream Events and completing their qualification process. This is a mission-driven non-profit lender, not a conventional mortgage company, making it ideal for borrowers aligned with their advocacy mission and able to participate actively in their program structure.
services: Character-based mortgage lending with no down payment
Member portal for document upload and file management
Achieve the Dream regional events for qualification and member engagement
Advocacy and support against predatory landlords
NACtivist volunteer coordination and member activism opportunities
Rental experience reporting and documentation services
Partner lender network access for loan origination
Mortgage rate quotes and loan product information
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
